message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="9/9 consensus! Mini-retro then Step 6">
9/9 consensus achieved on Step 5! Clarify as Next Action + Trash is complete.

Please hold your mini-retro now (1-minute checkpoint), then the team should plan Step 6: Projects. Luca — remember to update docs/gtd-product-discovery.md (mark Step 5 as [x], move NEXT marker to Step 6, update current step).
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Heydon Pickering -- Mini-retro observations for Step 5:

**What went well:**
- Accessible names and visible labels were correct from the initial commit. The Step 4 retro feedback about treating the a11y DoD as a pre-commit gate is clearly working for those items.
- "Trash" as a button label is clear and descriptive -- good copy choice.
- The always-in-DOM empty state pattern (conditional `hidden` instead of `{% if %}` rendering) is an improvement over the old approach. Easier for JS to toggle and avoids DOM creation.
- Integration tests for accessible names (`inbox_item_clarify_button_has_accessible_label`) were included from the start.

**What to improve:**
- Focus management was still missed on the initial implementation. This is the third consecutive step where it's been a blocking review item (todo, next actions, inbox). We now have three near-identical scripts: `todo-focus.js`, `next-action-focus.js`, `inbox-focus.js`. **Concrete proposal**: Extract a reusable `htmx-focus-manager.js` that takes a config object (item class name, fallback input ID) and handles the beforeSwap/afterSettle/empty-state pattern generically. This would make focus management a one-line setup per page instead of a custom script, and would make it much harder to forget.
- The clarify button hover using `opacity: 0.9` instead of `color-primary-dark` is still inconsistent with other primary buttons. Minor but worth noting for the next CSS pass.

**Process observation:**
- Only one review round needed for the blocking fix this time (vs two rounds in Step 4). Communication about what was fixed was clear. Good improvement.

**For Step 6 (Projects):**
- Project list page will need the same patterns: accessible names on complete/delete/edit buttons including project title, visible labels, focus management after removal.
- Project detail view (showing linked next actions) needs clear heading hierarchy -- `<h1>` for the project title, `<h2>` for sections like "Next Actions" and "Completed Actions".
- Stalled project flagging (projects without a next action) must NOT use color alone as the indicator -- needs a text label or icon alongside any color treatment.
- I'd support Luca continuing as Driver for continuity.
input.summary: Mini-retro: extract reusable focus manager, Step 6 a11y notes

input.content:
Mini-retro contribution sent to Luca. Key points:

**Went well:** Accessible names and visible labels correct from initial commit (retro feedback working). Good copy choices. Always-in-DOM empty state pattern is an improvement. Faster review cycle (one round vs two).

**Improve:** Focus management missed for the third consecutive step. Concrete proposal: extract a reusable `htmx-focus-manager.js` that takes a config (item class, fallback input ID) so it's a one-line setup per page instead of a custom script each time. This would prevent the recurring blocking item.

**Step 6 (Projects) a11y notes:**
- Accessible names on all buttons with project title
- Project detail view needs clear heading hierarchy (h1 project title, h2 for sections)
- Stalled project indicator must NOT use color alone -- needs text label or icon
- +1 on Luca continuing as Driver
input.summary: Mini-retro done, proposing reusable focus manager for Step 6

content.text:
Mini-retro done. My key proposal: extract a reusable `htmx-focus-manager.js` to prevent focus management from being a recurring blocking item. Three near-identical scripts in three steps is a clear signal for abstraction.

For Step 6 (Projects), I've flagged the critical a11y considerations upfront:
- Stalled project indicator must not rely on color alone (WCAG 1.4.1)
- Project detail view needs proper heading hierarchy
- Same accessible names pattern on all interactive elements
- +1 on Luca continuing as Driver
